*My Spoiler Review :-*


This is the best movie ever of all the Spiderman Movies ❤️First of all i want to thank Sony and Marvel to gave us best movie ever in spiderman universe 🥳 Hats of them🔥 My favourite spiderman is Tobey and Andrew and i didn't like tom Holland spiderman before this film as much but after i have watched this film my respect for Tom Holland's spiderman is increased 😇He nailed the role totally🔥 and when Andrew and Tobey came 😍OMG what a moment that was.. Literally the whole auditorium converted into stadium 😍😂 That was epic scene ever 🥳 I mean i was a fan of both of them since childhood and first time when i saw him together 😍My heart gone melt😭  After that they all 3 were talking  together.. 😍I was watching to them instead of listening dialogues😂i missed some dialogues due to watching them❤️😎 and after that when they were flying together ❤️ what a moment.. Its like dream come true 😇and also the jokes Cracks between them when they were in bridge.. Beautiful 😂😂❤️ When Tobey said "Avengers That's great, but what are they😂and Andrew said, are they band or something, are you part of rock band" 😂 I can't stop laughing that time 😂😂  and the villans also did amazing job in this film.. Specially the green goblin nailed the role in proper villan because green goblin was a only villan who portrayed bad till the end and other villans had become good at the end and yes I like DOC OCK in a good shade😂 and the fighting scene between him and spiderman was Lit🔥electro also nailed the character.. He said a dialogue that he like this universe and he wants to live here 😂I think marvel would plan with him to do a MCU movie or it's the reference for for "THE AMAZING SPIDER 3" maybe😂which i really want andrew to do 3rd part of "TASM" fingers crossed 🤭 and yes then scene when all the Villans talking to each other that was awesome and Golden scene 😂❤️thet was best comedy between villans 😂The whole film waa good perfect ❤️and Tom Holland Spiderman did justice to the role in this.. Because a spiderman known for his good work and sympathy to others who can't see other people's pain that's why he wants to fix the villans and he did also ❤️ But i am feeling bad for peter, now nobody knows him in his world 🥺 that was so emotional scene.. How he will survive without his own identity🥺 That sad.. But as his Aunt May and Tobey's Uncle Ben said "Great Power comes with great responsibility" ❤️ he have to survive with great responsibility 🥺
After that we lost Aunt May😭 that was so emotional scene.. I cried literally 🥺 We love Aunt may character ❤️ 

*Epic Film🥺😍*
That's my spoiler review 🥺

In my opinion, after watching in the cinema, the execution of the no way home film with the multiverse theme is very good, in terms of story plot, Jon wats the director not only presents the meeting of three spidey and the villains by force in order to quench the thirst of the audience, but the multiverse is neatly wrapped both in terms of historical and character development of all actors, from an emotional point of view, no way home expressly wants to bring us into a mixture of various models of feelings, sad, upset, angry and a little spice of dry jokes from the three spideys that make us unconsciously sometimes smile to ourselves. That's a bit of my impression after watching No Way Home, but above all I have a little constructive criticism of this film, sometimes too much CGI effect can also make the film seem tacky and feels animated, maybe that's a little lacking that I see from this film, but above s All of that, for me a Spidey fan since the Tobey Miguare era, No Way Home is like an "oasis" in the middle of the desert, successfully igniting a thirst for satisfaction by providing a kind of reunion for all generations of Spidey viewers. My advice, satisfy your emotions by watching this film in the cinema, consider this a Christmas gift from the MCU for all of us. MAY GOD BLESS

*I watched the movie three times and I would like to say that it really, really moved me. In the end, I felt that Tom Holland's Spider-Man stopped being a teenager and will finally face adult challenges. Enough of technology, he'll sew his own uniform, live alone and mourn Aunt May. I grew up watching Tobey Maguire's Spider-Man and am in love with Tom Holland's Peter. Hopefully, Marvel and Sony will make a new trilogy featuring Venom, Scorpio, Kraven, Black Cat etc (I hope so). And to see that the essence of Spider-Man / Peter Parker is saving the bad guys rather than killing them is beautiful; Spider-Man is a hero who wants to bring hope and be your friend, not hurt people, even bad people. I was extremely emotional when Tobey stopped Tom Holland from killing the Green Goblin, it's like he was saying: "Don't cross that line, you're better than this, we're better than this." I will never forget this movie. Thank you.*

Honestly, this movie wasn’t as good as I imagined. To Tom Holland fans, this was probably amazing. But as a Tobey Maguire & Andrew Garfield fan this could have been much better. I liked what they did for Andrew and Tobey showing the perfect mature spiderman and not allowing Tom to make the mistake he did. But that was it. The only fight scene that is worth mentioning is the green goblin vs Tom fight. That was the only good fight. The “big fight” was lame as hell. It was so rushed. There’s so much history between these characters and they rushed it. I would have rather had them introduce everyone and then make a separate movie instead of shoving everything in 2 1/2 hours. This is a mistake so many make. They hype the hell out of the movie and then they ruin it by rushing it. I’ve never been a fan of Tom Holland because he’s the weakest of them all and he’s always had help from someone or his stupid high tech suit. I’m glad Aunt May died and he lost his friends. Finally he’ll realize what Toby & Andrew had to go through. We might finally get to see a new side to this Tom Holland spiderman. Sorry for the rant. You may not agree with me and that’s okay. But I will say, I am grateful for them finally bringing back Tobey & Andrew

When people ask me why Spider-Man is my favorite superhero, I'm gonna tell them to watch this movie. 

Peter has lost so much, and he has always given his all when trying to save the day. However, we're reminded that Peter Parker is just a kid from Queens (again). He still has a long future ahead of him. Even though he sometimes makes the wrong choice, he always tries to make up for it. He sacrifices so much, to save everyone he cares about. There are many sad moments in the film, but I didn't start crying until the end when I realized what Peter was doing. That moment is why I love Spider-Man a.k.a Peter Parker. He can be so relatable at times and then there are these moments (like that one scene) where you see how selfless he is and how much he cares for everyone. This movie specifically tells the audience why Spider-Man is the greatest superhero ever and why he is so loved. 

I have no idea what the future holds for Spider-Man, but I'll be there 😉

Electro's solution was really simple with all of his electricity powers being absorbed into a little gadget on his chest, nothing too difficult for Peter.
